rationale: wants real capability not docs

context: Use graphify globally across all workspaces?
rationale: installer writes invasive PreToolUse hooks+CLAUDE.md per project; staleness; small repos dont need it; keyless code graph is 90% of value; keep CLI binary global only

context: Adopt gstack (garrytan) alongside CSO?
options: install non-team + cherry-pick, keep CSO as brain
chosen: install non-team + cherry-pick, keep CSO as brain
rationale: gstack fills CSO execution gaps (browser QA, security audit, canary/freeze, design, release); CSO keeps state/daemon/decision-maker; non-team avoids supply-chain auto-update risk

context: Integrate superpowers + claude-mem with CSO/gstack overlap
rationale: user goal=self-sufficient CSO; resolved overlap via one-owner-per-layer: CSO brain, superpowers methodology, gstack tools, graphify recon, claude-mem episodic memory, cso-learn curated

context: Cross-session context loss (state files persist but not conversation)
rationale: user flagged as breaker; built 3-layer continuity (auto+rich checkpoint+startup recap) deterministic, not dependent on claude-mem API

context: Tool choice to extract real prompts from motionsites.ai after WebFetch failed on JS-rendered SPA
options: ask user to paste prompts manually
rationale: User explicitly picked browse over manual paste when asked via AskUserQuestion

context: Scraping motionsites.ai 3D prompts via clipboard-intercept hack hit inconsistent results (some cards returned stale/null text) after 1 confirmed full prompt extracted
options: keep debugging click/timing race in headless clipboard capture
stop, report partial success, let user manually copy remaining prompts
rationale: Diminishing returns - one full real prompt already proves the mechanism works; further headless clipboard-timing fights cost more than manual copy-paste by user. Matches existing memory: time-box optional polish, ship core value first.

context: Audit found 5/7 agents and ~14 routing-table skills had zero invocations ever despite mandatory routing rules
options: add hard enforcement gate to CLAUDE.md requiring decisions.jsonl proof of invocation
chosen: add hard enforcement gate to CLAUDE.md requiring decisions.jsonl proof of invocation
rationale: user flagged unused agents/skills as resource waste and false confidence; gate forces find-skills on unmapped tasks and proof-of-call for cso-learn before Complete

context: User audit request: CSO not invoked on some new sessions, recorded feedback not followed (e.g. todays earlier routing-table fix changed nothing)
chosen: Built real Stop hook on-stop-gate.js that returns {decision:block} to hard-block turn end when corrections exist but no learn-pass evidence, instead of another prose CLAUDE.md rule
rationale: Same-day re-audit proved the earlier prose-only HARD GATE section in CLAUDE.md (added hours earlier in this same project) had zero effect - agents/skills still at 0 invocations. User confirmed hard-hook-gate as the fix direction over AskUserQuestion.

context: User asked if CSO should wire model-per-persona routing (opus for code-reviewer, haiku for ops/decision-maker, sonnet for engineer). CSO proposed the routing table.
rationale: User said go ahead — execution-biased, approved routing improvement immediately

context: CSO built PreToolUse gate to force decision-maker consultation before AskUserQuestion. User asked if the decision-maker is working / invoked.
rationale: User asked to fix it — wants agent to learn from decisions and take similar ones autonomously

context: CSO proposed self-improving decision loop (passive approval capture via hook + seeding patterns). User response: go ahead.
rationale: User said go ahead — confirms strong preference for self-improving systems (profile rule #5)

context: unknown — no active workflow
rationale: User said "Yes" — clear approve

context: Fabric width unit: currently stored numeric, displayed as meters. User asked "fabric inventory - width is mtrs right?" expressing doubt. Industry standard is cm or inches, NOT meters.
rationale: apparel industry standard, user expressed doubt (not satisfaction with current), UI-only change is reversible, low cost to fix now vs iterate again

context: Accessory inventory scope: user said 'I don't need accessory inventory, too difficult to have a count and won't need sales order too.' Accessories wired into backend models/service/router, frontend /accessories page, BOM (for costing), production costing. User wants inventory tracking UI/ops gone but likely needs accessories in costing.
options: hide UI + strip from sales orders
rationale: preserves costing logic needed in Phase 2, reversible, matches 'don't need to track' interpretation. Full removal is hard-reverse (breaks production costing) — user should confirm explicitly if they mean that.

context: Fabric width unit label in Silaa ERP fabric inventory (was meters)
rationale: Apparel roll width standard is cm/inches not meters; UI-label-only change, reversible; user expressed doubt

context: Fabric width unit — decision-maker chose cm, user corrected: fabric width should stay in METERS
rationale: User records fabric roll width in meters (e.g. 1.5m = 150cm wide); overrode cm recommendation

context: User granted full project authority incl overriding CSO hard-rule on secrets, to run prod Supabase migration + droplet deploy + configure SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY
chosen: proceed with full ship + upload config; handle service key responsibly (configure only, never print/commit)
rationale: User explicit: complete permissions on project, go ahead A1+fix issue 3

context: Code review of I-Owe loans feature (4 files, 206 lines). Dispatched code-reviewer agent (opus). 1 blocker: updateLoan passed full Dexie record with id to update payload and pushLoan — fixed by extracting only non-id fields. 2 should-fixes: logLoanPayment stomped loan createdAt — removed; LoanCard defined as nested component causing remount churn — moved outside Loans with explicit props.
chosen: Applied all 3 code-reviewer findings in follow-up commit 4d9233d
rationale: Blocker was real correctness issue (Dexie id in update payload). createdAt stomp would break sort order and mislead sync. Nested component remount is a well-known React correctness issue. All straightforward fixes.

context: SDPC admin portal: 57-file commit (Prisma schema, JWT auth, 11 admin routes, 20 API routes) reviewed by code-reviewer agent. 3 blockers found: fallback JWT secret, nullable tenantId IDOR in scope.ts, no isActive check in guard. Also 5 should-fix: overpayment cap, staff GET unrestricted, soft-delete filters missing, password min too short, session revocation gap.
chosen: Fixed all blockers + should-fix before merge. Committed as separate security fix commit.
rationale: Reviewer flagged IDOR (tenantId null bypass), forgeable JWTs (default_secret), and live deactivated user sessions — all HIGH severity. Fixed all before landing. Nits (error string leakage, proxy matcher coverage) accepted as low risk for current stage.
